fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alabama Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity alabama, how to transfer dietitian license to alabama, dietitian compact alabama, alabama dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Alabama Board of Examiners for Dietetics/Nutrition Practice

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD) / Licensed Nutritionist (LN)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice dietetics in Alabama. Alabama law protects both the 'Dietitian' and 'Nutritionist' titles.

license_types.title: Temporary License
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have met the educational and supervised practice requirements and are preparing to take the CDR exam. Valid for one year. Fee is $175.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of supervised practice.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

steps.title: Submit the Application
steps.description: Complete the licensure application and submit it to the Board with the $100 application fee and $150 initial license fee ($250 total).

steps.title: Provide Verification
steps.description: Submit a copy of your current, active CDR registration card or verification letter directly from CDR.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Verification of identity and legal presence in the U.S.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

provisional_text: Alabama offers a temporary license for $175 for individuals who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. It is valid for one year.
reciprocity_text: Alabama allows licensure by endorsement for dietitians who hold a current, valid license in another state with requirements equivalent to or stricter than Alabama's, provided the applicant holds active CDR registration.

faqs.question: How much does an Alabama dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial cost is $250, which includes a non-refundable $100 application fee and a $150 license fee. The temporary license costs $175.

faqs.question: When does the Alabama license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: The license is renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $200.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements?
faqs.answer: Alabama requires 30 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) during each two-year renewal cycle.

faqs.question: Does Alabama have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Alabama. To work in Alabama, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Alabama allows licensure by endorsement for dietitians who hold a current, valid license in another state with requirements equivalent to or stricter than Alabama's, provided the applicant holds active CDR registration.
